metaDescription: Learn how to export your Freshdesk tickets to a file so you can analyze data, back it up, or share it outside the platform.
intro: Sometimes you need ticket data outside of Freshdesk for reporting or record-keeping. This guide covers how to export your tickets so you can work with them in a spreadsheet or another tool.

steps.heading: Go to the tickets list
steps.body: Navigate to the ticket view containing the tickets you want to export.

steps.heading: Apply filters if needed
steps.body: Narrow down the tickets by date range, status, or other criteria before exporting to keep the file focused.

steps.heading: Select the export option
steps.body: Use the export action available in the ticket list to generate a downloadable file.

steps.heading: Download and open the file
steps.body: Once the export is ready, download it and open it in a spreadsheet application to review the data.

faq.question: What file format do Freshdesk exports use?
faq.answer: Exports are typically provided as CSV files that open easily in spreadsheet software.

faq.question: Can I export only specific tickets?
faq.answer: Yes, applying filters before exporting lets you limit the export to the tickets you actually need.

faq.question: Is there a limit to how many tickets I can export at once?
faq.answer: Export limits can vary depending on your Freshdesk plan and ticket volume, so check your account settings for specifics.

faq.question: Can I schedule automatic exports?
faq.answer: Some Freshdesk plans support scheduled reports, which can serve a similar purpose to manual exports.
